description_long: This object is a cluster of delicate, light purple flowers with prominent yellow centers, each supported by slender green stems. The flowers are arranged above a dense base of dark green, serrated leaves that radiate outward, giving the plant a bushy appearance. The petals are broad, slightly overlapping, and arranged in a radial symmetry around the central disc. The stems are thin and upright, varying slightly in height, and the leaves are lanceolate with sharply toothed edges. The overall structure is airy and open, with the flowers elevated above the foliage. This type of plant is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or roadside planters, adding a touch of natural beauty to the environment.
description: A cluster of light purple, yellow-centered flowers with serrated dark green leaves and slender stems, arranged in a bushy, upright form. The petals are broad and radially symmetrical, giving the plant a delicate and ornamental appearance.
description_view_0: From this view, three prominent flowers face forward, showing their yellow centers and broad, overlapping petals, with dense foliage visible at the base.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the side profile of the flowers, revealing the layering of petals and the spread of the serrated leaves outward from the stems.
description_view_2: The front-facing flowers are most visible here, with their centers and petal arrangement clearly displayed, and the foliage appearing more compact.
description_view_3: This view shows the flowers from a slightly elevated angle, emphasizing the spread and height variation of the stems and the bushy arrangement of the leaves.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration around the central disc, with slight overlapping and clear radial symmetry typical of daisy-like flowers.
